foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Splenic anatomy shares peritoneal ligaments with the left lobe of liver; understanding is needed for splenectomy mobilization

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Splenomegaly from portal hypertension is a major indication for splenectomy and causes hypersplenism

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Hypersplenism secondary to cirrhosis-related portal hypertension impacts platelet counts affecting HCC treatment decisions

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Pigment gallstones from chronic hemolysis in hereditary spherocytosis often necessitate concurrent cholecystectomy with splenectomy

objectives: SU28.11: Describe the applied anatomy of Spleen. Describe the clinical features,
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Spleen — Surgical Conditions across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
